Notes
Pending legislation includes legislation that has passed one or both chambers.

Under current law, states must provide continuous coverage to Medicaid enrollees through the public health emergency (PHE) period to be eligible for enhanced federal matching funds under the Families First Coronavirus Response Act. States seeking to implement extended postpartum coverage after the end of the PHE but prior to April 1, 2022 must do so through a Section 1115 waiver or by using state funds.
CMS approved the U.S. Virgin Island's [state plan amendment](https://www.medicaid.gov/medicaid/spa/downloads/VI-22-0004.pdf) on June 23, 2023.
Sources

KFF analysis of approved and pending 1115 waivers, state legislation, and Medicaid state plan amendments. For links to key documents, click [here](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/18qVwD_MK6IOHYWyo9qACCxSWVdE40l-Gq20p2Jix6WI/edit#gid=0).
Definitions

*Medicaid/CHIP Income Eligibility Limit for Pregnancy*: This column indicates the upper Medicaid/CHIP income eligibility limit for pregnant women, calculated as a percent of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL). See [here](https://www.kff.org/report-section/a-look-at-medicaid-and-chip-eligibility-enrollment-and-renewal-policies-during-the-unwinding-of-continuous-enrollment-and-beyond-appendix-tables/#Table3) for more information on Medicaid and CHIP income eligibility limits for pregnant women.

Illinois also received approval for a CHIP state plan amendment to implement a health services initiative (HSI) to extend postpartum coverage from 60 days to 12 months under the CHIP unborn child option. This coverage applies to postpartum individuals who are not eligible for Medicaid due to immigration status.

14. In June 2021, Indiana withdrew a pending 1115 waiver (Maternal Opioid Misuse Indiana Initiative) that would have extended postpartum coverage only to pregnant individuals with opioid use disorder. In a letter to CMS, the state announced its intention to pursue the broader postpartum coverage extension under the American Rescue Plan Act and CMS approved Indiana's state plan amendment on September 8, 2022.
